text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

James Porter, DOB: 22-07-1987, Hospital No: U914372 NHS No. 6382749105  
Flat 4, 19 Kingfisher Court, London, NW1 6QR 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Epilepsy with prior episodes of loss of awareness of uncertain classification; sleep disruption related to rotating shift work including nights; migraine without aura; vitamin D insufficiency; tobacco smoker; no current alcohol use. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily.  
Sumatriptan 50 mg as needed for migraine (max 2 doses/24 h).  
Cholecalciferol 1000 IU daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg daily.  

I reviewed James Porter in clinic today. He attended alone. He works alternating early, late and night shifts on a 6-week rota and reports persistent circadian disruption, with fragmented daytime sleep after nights and difficulty initiating sleep on transition weeks. Caffeine intake is approximately two strong coffees before night shifts; he denies energy drink use. Appetite is variable but weight has been stable. No intercurrent illness and no missed medication doses reported in the last three months; he uses a smartphone alarm to time doses across shift changes. 

Seizures: He and his partner maintain a shared calendar log; there have been no events suggestive of seizures since the previous appointment. He specifically denies unresponsiveness, witnessed convulsions, tongue biting, incontinence, nocturnal injuries, or prolonged post-event confusion. He has had occasional brief light-headed spells on post-night mornings that resolve with rest and fluids and are not associated with amnesia or automatisms. 

Examination: BP 128/76 mmHg, pulse 72 bpm regular. Weight 78 kg, BMI 24.9. Neurological examination normal: alert, oriented, fluent speech, cranial nerves intact, full power, normal tone and coordination, gait steady. 

Investigations: Routine bloods from GP (08/2025): FBC, U&E, LFTs within reference; vitamin D 54 nmol/L on supplementation. Previous MRI brain (2022) unremarkable. No new imaging indicated today. 

Discussion: We reviewed seizure risk in the context of disrupted sleep from rotating shift work. He understands that sleep deprivation can lower seizure threshold. He prefers to continue current employment but will discuss with occupational health practical adjustments to improve sleep opportunity. We discussed consistent medication timing relative to wake–sleep schedule and minimising large caffeine loads on transition days, which he will trial. 

Plan: 
- Continue levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily without change.  
- Maintain seizure and sleep diary (calendar app acceptable).  
- Liaise with workplace occupational health regarding rostering predictability and protected sleep periods between nights; patient to update us.  
- GP to recheck vitamin D and routine bloods in 6 months.  
- Safety advice reiterated regarding driving, swimming alone, heights, and operating machinery; he does not currently drive.  
- Neurology follow-up in 6 months, sooner if any events occur or tolerability issues arise.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: there have been no events suggestive of seizures since the previous appointment

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly addresses seizures: "there have been no events suggestive of seizures since the previous appointment." It does not provide the exact date of the previous appointment. For seizure-free labeling, at least 6 months of seizure freedom must be confirmed. Since the duration since the previous appointment is unspecified, we cannot confirm ≥6 months. However, the patient currently has no seizures and has been free since a prior visit of unknown timing, which fits 'seizure free for multiple months' per the instruction allowing this when duration is insufficient to confirm or refute 6 months.
seizure_frequency_number: there have been no events suggestive of seizures since the previous appointment
reference: No seizures reported since last evaluation
